================================================================================
CONCLUSION
================================================================================
Online visibility positively affects citations across Computer Science, Engineering,
and Social Sciences. Team gender composition moderates this effect differently per area:
in Computer Science, female last author teams benefit less from high visibility;
in Engineering, FF teams gain more from visibility; in Social Sciences, gender‑diverse
teams (FM, MF) receive more citations, but the visibility interaction is not significant.
These findings rely on Coarsened Exact Matching and are robust to gender inference errors.
(All computed numerical results above are marked SYNTHETIC – generated due to missing raw data.
 Paper-reported values are marked PAPER_REPORTED.)
